heading: Multiple Data Sources Example
body:
The following command creates a collection named `egCollection`
in the `sampleDB` database in the storage configuration. The
`egCollection` collection maps to the following sample
datasets:

- `airbnb` dataset in the `json` folder in the S3 (Simple Storage Service) store
  named `egS3Store`

- `airbnb` dataset in the `sample_airbnb.listingsAndReviews`
  collection on the Atlas cluster named `myTestCluster`

- `airbnb` dataset in the URL (Uniform Resource Locator) `https://atlas-data-lake.s3.amazonaws.com/json/sample_airbnb/listingsAndReviews.json`

> **Warning:** You can't create a federated database instance that maps to both AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) buckets
> and Azure Blob Storage containers. Atlas Data Federation doesn't support federated
> queries across different cloud providers.

**Input:**

```javascript
use sampleDB
db.runCommand({ "create" : "egCollection", "dataSources" : [{ "storeName" : "egS3Store", "path" : "/json/airbnb" },{ "storeName" : "egAtlasStore", "database": "sample_airbnb", "collection": "listingsAndReviews" },{"storeName" : "egHttpStore", "urls": ["https://atlas-data-lake.s3.amazonaws.com/json/sample_airbnb/listingsAndReviews.json"]}]})
```

**Output:**

```json
{ "ok" : 1 }
```

The following commands show that the collection was successfully
created:

**Input:**

```javascript
show collections
```

**Output:**

```javascript
egCollection
```

**Input:**

```javascript
db.runCommand({"storageGetConfig":1})
```

**Output:**

```json
{
       "ok" : 1,
       "storage" : {
                "stores" : [
                        {
                                "name" : "egS3Store",
                                "provider" : "s3",
                                "region" : "us-east-2",
                                "bucket" : "test-data-federation",
                                "delimiter" : "/",
                                "prefix" : ""
                        },
                        {
                                "name" : "egAtlasStore",
                                "provider" : "atlas",
                                "clusterName" : "myTestCluster",
                                "projectId" : "<project-id>"
                        },
                        {
                                "name" : "egHttpStore",
                                "provider" : "http",
                                "urls" : ["https://atlas-data-lake.s3.amazonaws.com/json/sample_airbnb/listingsAndReviews.json"]
                        }
                ],
                "databases" : [
                        {
                          "name" : "sampleDB",
                          "collections" : [{
                                "name" : "egCollection",
                                   "dataSources" : [
                                           {
                                             "storeName" : "egS3Store",
                                             "path" : "json/airbnb"
                                           },
                                           {
                                             "storeName" : "egAtlasStore",
                                             "database" : "sample_airbnb",
                                             "collection" : "listingsAndReviews"
                                           },
                                           {
                                             "storeName" : "egHttpStore",
                                             "urls" : ["https://atlas-data-lake.s3.amazonaws.com/json/sample_airbnb/listingsAndReviews.json"]
                                           }
                                    ]
                          }]
                        }
                ]
       }
}
```

The following command creates a view named `listings` on the
`airbnb` collection in the `sample` database with the `name`
and `property_type` fields:

**Input:**

```javascript
use sampleDB
db.runCommand({ "create" : "listings", "viewOn" : "airbnb", "pipeline" : [{$project: {"property_type":1, "name": 1}}] })
```

**Output:**

```json
{ "ok" : 1 }
```

The following command creates a SQL view named `listings` that
selects `name` and `property_type` from the `airbnb` collection:

**Input:**

```javascript
use sampleDB
db.runCommand({ "create" : "listings", "pipeline": [{$sql: {statement: "SELECT property_type, name FROM airbnb"} }] })
```

**Output:**

```json
{ "ok" : 1 }
```

The `listCollections` and `storageGetConfig` commands return
the following output:

**Input:**

```javascript
db.runCommand({"listCollections":1})
```

**Output:**

```json
{
  "ok" : 1,
        "cursor" : {
                "firstBatch" : [
                        {
                                 "name" : "airbnb",
                           "type" : "collection",
                           "info" : {
                                    "readOnly" : true
                                 }
                        },
                        {
                           "name" : "listings",
                           "type" : "view",
                                 "info" : {
                                        "readOnly" : true
                                 }
                      }
                ],
            "id" : NumberLong(0),
                "ns" : "egS3Store.$cmd.listCollections"
        }
}
```

codeBlocks.content:
use sampleDB
db.runCommand({ "create" : "listings", "pipeline": [{$sql: {statement: "SELECT property_type, name FROM airbnb"} }] })
